turns.content: screwfix have got a sale on and I am going in. need your help deciding what to get
A Screwfix sale is always dangerous for the wallet. What are you looking to pick up?

right so main thing is pipe cutters. my current ones are knackered. the blade is blunt and it crushes the copper instead of cutting it clean. but theres a few options and I want to get the right ones this time

Makes sense -- a clean cut is everything with copper pipe. What sizes are you mainly working with? That'll narrow down which cutter is the best fit. And what's your budget looking like with the sale prices?

mostly 15mm and 22mm which is standard domestic stuff. sometimes 28mm for mains connections but not that often. budget wise I dont mind spending a bit more if its quality but the whole point of buying in the sale is saving money innit. theyve got the Rothenberger ones on offer and also some Monument ones

Both are solid brands for pipe cutters. Rothenberger and Monument are the two names most plumbers trust. Here's how they compare:

**Rothenberger** -- generally considered the premium option. Their pipe cutters tend to have tighter tolerances, smoother wheel action, and the replacement blades are easy to source. The No.35 handles 6-35mm which would cover all your sizes in one tool.

**Monument** -- reliable workhorse, slightly cheaper. Their cutters are well-built and do the job cleanly. The blade quality is good but some plumbers find the wheel mechanism isn't quite as smooth as Rothenberger over time.

If the sale puts the Rothenberger close to the Monument's normal price, I'd go Rothenberger. What are the sale prices looking like?
rothenberger no.35 is down from 28 quid to 19. the monument one that does 15 and 22mm is 12 quid down from 16. so the rothenberger is still more but its a proper discount

Nineteen quid for a Rothenberger No.35 is a great deal -- that's over 30% off and you're getting a cutter that handles 6-35mm, so it covers everything including the 28mm mains work. At that price it's almost a no-brainer over the Monument, because you're paying seven quid more for a tool that covers a wider range and will likely last longer. The Monument at twelve quid is fine for a backup, but as your main cutter I'd go Rothenberger at that discount.

yeah thats what I was thinking. might get both honestly. have the monument in the tool bag as a spare and the rothenberger as the main one. nothing worse than your cutter dying mid job and having to drive to screwfix in the middle of the day

Having a backup cutter is smart -- thirty-one quid for both at sale price is less than what the Rothenberger alone would normally cost. The time and petrol you save by not having to make an emergency Screwfix run mid-job will pay for the Monument in no time. Are you picking up anything else while you're at it?

well thats the danger isnt it. you go in for one thing and come out with a trolley full. theyve got PTFE tape on offer too 10 rolls for 4 quid which is mental. and flux is discounted as well. might stock up on all the consumables while the prices are down

PTFE tape at ten rolls for four quid is a genuine steal -- that's the kind of thing you burn through constantly and it never goes off, so stocking up makes total sense. Same with flux if you're doing any soldered joints. Consumables at sale prices are always worth bulk buying because you know you'll use them. Just don't let the sale talk you into buying a new cordless drill you don't need.
haha dont tempt me. theyve got the makita impact driver at 20% off and I keep looking at it. I dont need it. I absolutely do not need it. but 20% off mate thats hard to walk past

The classic Screwfix trap -- you don't need it until you see the discount. If your current impact driver is working fine, you definitely don't need it. But if it's getting tired or you've been thinking about upgrading anyway, then 20% off a Makita is a legitimate deal. What's the honest state of your current one?

its fine. its completely fine. battery still holds charge and everything. im just being greedy arent I. right no drill. pipe cutters, PTFE tape, flux. in and out. minimal damage to the bank account. Simran will check the receipt anyway she always does haha

That's the disciplined move. Pipe cutters, PTFE tape, and flux -- all things you genuinely need, all at proper discounts. You'll come in well under fifty quid and everything you buy will earn its keep. Save the Makita for the next sale when your current one actually needs replacing. And good luck getting past receipt inspection.
